channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Baltic Oil Port of Primorsk in Major Drone Assault
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on targets across Russia, hitting the Baltic Sea port of Primorsk and setting it on fire, while striking multiple vessels. The attack on Russia's key oil export terminal marks a significant escalation in Ukraine's campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: IAEA Reports Drone Strike on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Radiation Lab
item.description: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant informed the International Atomic Energy Agency that a drone targeted its external radiation control laboratory. The incident raises fresh concerns about nuclear safety near the front lines of the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: Jailed Iranian Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Critical Condition
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in a cardiac care unit in northwestern Iran after being transferred from prison. Her family's foundation warns that authorities have blocked her transfer to Tehran for specialist care.

item.title: UAE Exits Arab Oil Exporters Group OAPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has left the Organization of Arab Petroleum Exporting Countries (OAPEC), an alliance that does not set production policies. The move comes amid shifting energy dynamics in the Gulf region following the Iran-US conflict.

item.title: Japanese Yen Steadies as Intervention Fears Linger
item.description: The yen stabilized at the start of Asian trading, strengthening slightly after suspected currency intervention by Japanese authorities last week. Markets remain on alert for further action as the Iran conflict continues to impact global financial markets.

item.title: Iran Warns US Hormuz Operation Would Violate Ceasefire
item.description: A senior Iranian parliament official stated that any US interference in the Strait of Hormuz maritime regime would be considered a ceasefire violation. CENTCOM said the operation would involve destroyers, 100+ aircraft, and 15,000 service members.

item.title: OPEC+ Agrees Third Output Quota Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ has agreed to a third increase in oil production quotas since the Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed due to the Iran war. The increase remains largely theoretical as long as Gulf oil shipments are disrupted.

item.title: Oil Prices Ease After Trump Hormuz Escort Announcement
item.description: Oil prices fell after President Trump announced the US would help free ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, providing some relief to markets concerned about global energy supplies.

item.title: Israel Approves Purchase of New F-35 and F-15IA Fighter Squadrons
item.description: Israel has approved a plan to purchase new F-35 and F-15IA fighter aircraft from the United States as part of a major defense modernization program. The deal comes amid heightened regional tensions following the Iran conflict.

item.title: Iranian Drone Strike Hits Oman's Largest Oil Storage Facility
item.description: An Iranian drone struck Oman's largest oil storage facility at the port of Salalah, marking an expansion of the conflict beyond the Strait of Hormuz. The attack threatens global oil supplies transiting through alternative routes.

item.title: Oil Surges Past $120/Barrel as Trump Rejects Iran Overture
item.description: Oil prices surged past $120 per barrel, reaching war-time highs, after President Trump rejected an Iranian diplomatic overture. The rejection signals continued tensions in the Gulf region with no immediate end to supply disruptions.

item.title: Pentagon's Office of Net Assessment Chief Joins Anthropic
item.description: The former head of the Pentagon's Office of Net Assessment has joined AI company Anthropic, marking a significant shift from defense policy to the artificial intelligence industry.

item.title: China Stages Naval Drill East of Philippines as US-Balikatan Exercises Continue
item.description: China conducted naval exercises east of the Philippines while the US and Philippines continued their Balikatan joint military exercises. The simultaneous drills highlight growing military tensions in the South China Sea region.

item.title: Trump Announces "Project Freedom" to Free Ships from Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced "Project Freedom," an operation to escort commercial ships out of the Strait of Hormuz. The effort aims to free vessels stranded since Iran effectively closed the strategic waterway.

item.title: Fed Kashkari: Iran War Limits Rate Guidance
item.description: Minneapolis Federal Reserve President Neel Kashkari said the Iran war limits the Fed's ability to provide guidance on interest rates, adding uncertainty about inflation and economic growth trajectories.

item.title: China Seeks Advantage Between Trump and Iran as War Evolves
item.description: China is positioning itself to extract strategic advantage from the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran, balancing its relationships with both nations while seeking to protect its energy interests in the Gulf region.

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio to Meet Pope Leo This Week
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to the Vatican and Italy this week for meetings with Pope Leo and Italian officials, according to newspaper reports.

item.title: UK to Join EU 105 Billion Dollar Ukraine Loan
item.description: The United Kingdom is set to enter talks to join the European Union's 105 billion dollar loan package for Ukraine, deepening Western financial support for Kyiv amid the ongoing conflict with Russia.

item.title: Two US Troops Missing During African Lion Military Exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two US service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, according to US Africa Command and Moroccan Royal Armed Forces.

item.title: Trump Administration Halts US Wind Farm Projects Citing National Security
item.description: The Trump administration has halted US wind farm projects, citing national security concerns. The move represents a significant shift in energy policy and could impact renewable energy development.

item.title: Israel Court Extends Detention of Gaza Flotilla Activists
item.description: An Israeli court extended the detention of two activists arrested aboard a Gaza-bound flotilla that was intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters near Greece. The activists were attempting to break the Gaza blockade.

item.title: ECB Policymaker Warns Euro Zone Recession Concerns Are Real and Justified
item.description: Bank of Greece Governor Yannis Stournaras warned that concerns about a euro zone recession are "real and justified" if the Middle East conflict continues, adding that Iran war talks will be key for ECB monetary policy decisions.

item.title: ADB Launches Finance Scheme for Critical Mineral Supply Chains in Asia-Pacific
item.description: The Asian Development Bank has launched a new financing scheme to support critical mineral supply chains in the Asia-Pacific region, addressing strategic resource security amid growing global competition.

item.title: Top Senate Armed Services Democrat: No Timeouts on Iran War Powers
item.description: Senator Jack Reed, the top Democrat on the Senate Armed Services Committee, criticized the administration's handling of Iran war powers, stating there should be no timeouts on congressional oversight of military actions.

item.title: House Democrat Calls Germany Troop Withdrawal No Way to Run Foreign Policy
item.description: House Democrat Jason Crow criticized the planned withdrawal of US troops from Germany, saying it is no way to run foreign policy and raises concerns about NATO alliance commitments.
